title: Asclepias rectinervis
taxonomy: Asclepias rectinervis is a species of milkweed plant, classified in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Asclepias, which comprises over 1 hundred species of milkweed plants. The species Asclepias rectinervis is native to Mexico and is closely related to Asclepias erosa and Asclepias cordifolia. Asclepias rectinervis is further categorized as Asclepias subgenus Asclepias. The classification of Asclepias rectinervis is also recognized in the order Gentianales.

intro: Asclepias rectinervis, also known as the straight-veined milkweed, is a perennial plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Asclepias rectinervis include cardenolides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Asclepias rectinervis is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, particularly in traditional folk medicine.
